author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again, on the Lord’s Prayer, Matt. vi. To the Competentes.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Again, on the Lord’s Prayer, Matt. vi. To the Competentes.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have rehearsed what you believe, hear now what you are to pray for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch as you would not be able to call on Him, in whom you should not first have believed; as saith the Apostle, “How shall they call on Him, in whom they have not believed?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore have you first learned the Creed, where is a brief and sublime rule of your faith; brief in the number of its words, sublime in the weight of its contents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the prayer which you receive to-day to be learned by heart, and to be repeated eight days hence, was dictated (as you heard when the Gospel was being read) by the Lord Himself to His disciples, and came from them unto us, since “their sound went into all the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye then who have found a Father in heaven, be loth to cleave to the things of earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye are about to say, “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You have begun to belong to a great family.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Under this Father the lord and the slave are brethren; under this Father the general and the common soldier are brethren; under this Father the rich man and the poor are brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All Christian believers have divers fathers in earth, some noble, some obscure; but they all call upon one Father which is in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If our Father be there, there is the inheritance prepared for us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He is such a Father, that we can possess with Him what He giveth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He giveth an inheritance; but He doth not leave it to us by dying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He doth not depart Himself, but He abideth ever, that we may come to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seeing then we have heard of Whom we are to ask, let us know also what to ask for, lest haply we offend such a Father by asking amiss.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then hath the Lord Jesus Christ taught us to ask of the Father which is in heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hallowed be Thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What kind of blessing is this that we ask of God, that His Name may be hallowed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Name of God is always Holy; why then do we pray that it may be hallowed, except that we may be hallowed by it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We pray then that that which is Holy always, may be hallowed in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Name of God is hallowed in you when ye are baptized.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why will ye offer this prayer after ye have been baptized, but that that which ye shall then receive may abide ever in you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another petition follows, “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God’s kingdom will come, whether we ask it or not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then do we ask it, but that that which will come to all saints may also come to us; that God may count us also in the number of His saints, to whom His kingdom is to come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We say in the third petition, “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That as the Angels serve Thee in heaven, so we may serve Thee in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For His holy Angels obey Him; they do not offend Him; they do His commands through the love of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we pray for then, that we too may do the commands of God in love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, these words are understood in another way, “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Heaven in us is the soul, earth in us is the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As we hear Thy precepts, so may our flesh consent unto us; lest, whilst flesh and spirit strive together, we be not able to fulfil the commands of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give us this day our daily bread,” comes next in the Prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether we ask here of the Father support necessary for the body, by “bread” signifying whatever is needful for us; or whether we understand that daily Bread, which ye are soon to receive from the Altar; well it is that we pray that He would give it us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is it we pray for, but that we may commit no evil, for which we should be separated from that holy Bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the word of God which is preached daily is daily bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For because it is not bread for the body, it is not on that account not bread for the soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when this life shall have passed away, we shall neither seek that bread which hunger seeks; nor shall we have to receive the Sacrament of the Altar, because we shall be there with Christ, whose Body we do now receive; nor will those words which we are now speaking, need to be said to you, nor the sacred volume to be read, when we shall see Him who is Himself the Word of God, by whom all things were made, by whom the Angels are fed, by whom the Angels are enlightened, by whom the Angels become wise; not requiring words of circuitous discourse; but drinking in the Only Word, filled with whom they burst forth and never fail in praise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “Blessed,” saith the Psalm, “are they who dwell in Thy house; they will be always praising Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in this present life, do we ask what comes next, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In Baptism, all debts, that is, all sins, are entirely forgiven us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because no one can live without sin here below, and if without any great crime which entails separation from the Altar, yet altogether without sins can no one live on this earth, and we can only receive the one Baptism once for all; in this Prayer we hear how we may day by day be washed, that our sins may day by day be forgiven us; but only if we do what follows, “As we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly, my Brethren, I advise you, who are in the grace of God my sons, yet my Brethren under that heavenly Father; I advise you, whenever any one offends and sins against you, and comes, and confesses, and asks your pardon, that ye do pardon him, and forthwith from the heart forgive him; lest ye keep off from your own selves that pardon, which comes from God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if ye forgive not, neither will He forgive you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore it is in this life that we make this petition, for that it is in this life that sins can be forgiven, where they can be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in the life to come they are not forgiven, because they are not done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Next after this we pray, saying, “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This also, that we be not led into temptation, it is necessary for us to ask in this life, because in this life there are temptations; and that “we may be delivered from evil,” because there is evil here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thus of all these seven petitions, three have respect to the life eternal, and four to the present life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hallowed be Thy name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This kingdom will be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will not be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will not be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will not be for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This will not be for ever: but where there is temptation, and where there is evil, there is it necessary that we make this petition.
